title: Podman Machine start Hangs with Hyper-V on Win 11 ARM64
body: ### Issue Description I have been trying to start the default podman machine on my Windows 11 ARM64 laptop, the WSL2 issue with creating the machine was fixed [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25621). However during that testing I noticed using Hyper-V creates the default podman machine successfully, but never can start the VM. ``` $ podman machine init Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.4 to create VM Getting image source signatures Copying blob 069afc783a38 done | Copying config 44136fa355 done | Writing manifest to image destination 069afc783a38484751aa451c7316e7b68b2c5f5ed7d90e712a43f1bf04566591 Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-amd64.vhdx: done Machine init complete To start your machine run: podman machine start ``` `podman machine start` hangs indefinitely and when looking at the VM's console is displays this error: I also tried the dev podman installer in this [issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25621) and it ends up going into emergency mode on trying to start the machine: ### Steps to reproduce the issue Install Podman and/or Podman Desktop on WIndows 11 ARM64 using Hyper-V instead of WSL2 and attempt to create the default podman VM. View the VM from the Hyper-V manager and check VM for errors. ### Describe the results you received I created the default podman machine successfully with `podman machine init` with Hyper-V backing and then `podman machine start` never completed, the VM could not boot ### Describe the results you expected I expect upon successfully creating the default podman machine that the machine starts and podman runs as expected. ### podman info output ```yaml podman version 5.4.2 Windows 11 24H2 (OS Build 26100.3194) ARM 64 ``` ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless Privileged ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details Additional environment details ### Additional information _No response_

comments.body:
> Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-amd64.vhdx: done

Looks like it is pulling the wrong image architecture.
cc @baude

Oh wow I probably shouldn't have missed that, fwiw the dev 5.5 podman installer looks to grab the correct arch:
```
$ podman machine init                                                             
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.5 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob f8947dc99195 done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
f8947dc99195a5646f93aab9ee55adc189f1bd60aca2554beaa60a05d288f4b1
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-arm64.vhdx: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start
```

Could be related to this issue, as it seems like a similar symptom: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25621

Edit: I see these issues have already been called out as related. :)
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Note we have proper arm64 windows installers now, see podman-installer-windows-arm64.exe on the release page https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.5.0

Please see if that changed anything.
Thank you, 5.5 has resolved the issue, closing.
